title: Apply for the CLA Foundation Scholarship 2026 and Receive $15,000 for Your Studies
summary: The CLA Foundation Opportunity Scholarship 2026 offers students across the United States the chance to receive $15,000 in funding to support their undergraduate or graduate studies in business, finance, technology, and other emerging professional fields. Open to high school seniors, current college students, and graduate students, this scholarship aims to support future leaders shaping the evolving landscape of professional services. Administered by Scholarship America, the program identifies high-potential students who demonstrate academic achievement, leadership, and a clear commitment to their chosen career path. With applications open until May 19, 2026 , this opportunity provides financial support while helping students advance toward careers in high-demand sectors. About the Program The CLA Foundation Opportunity Scholarship Program is designed to invest in students pursuing careers in both traditional and emerging professional service industries. These include fields such as accounting, finance, and business, as well as rapidly growing areas like artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, and regulatory technology. The scholarship is funded by the CLA Foundation and administered by Scholarship America, one of the largest scholarship management organizations in the United States. Through this collaboration, the program ensures a rigorous and fair selection process, identifying students who demonstrate both academic excellence and future leadership potential. Recipients are awarded a one-time scholarship of $15,000, which can be used to support tuition and other educational expenses at accredited two-year or four-year colleges, universities, or graduate schools. Why This Opportunity Matters The professional services sector is undergoing rapid transformation due to advancements in technology and shifting global demands. Fields such as artificial intelligence, data analytics, and digital transformation are reshaping how businesses operate, creating new opportunities for skilled professionals. This scholarship supports students entering these evolving fields by reducing financial barriers and enabling them to focus on their education and career development. By investing in both traditional and emerging disciplines, the program helps prepare a new generation of professionals equipped to lead innovation and drive economic growth. In addition, the scholarship recognizes the importance of leadership, community involvement, and resilience. By selecting students who demonstrate these qualities, the program contributes to building a workforce that is not only skilled but also socially responsible and community-oriented. What Participants Will Gain The CLA Foundation Opportunity Scholarship provides both financial and professional advantages. Recipients receive $15,000 in funding, which can significantly reduce the cost of higher education and ease financial stress. Beyond financial support, the scholarship enhances students’ academic and professional profiles. Being selected as a recipient signals strong leadership potential and academic achievement, which can open doors to future opportunities. Participants may also gain access to internship opportunities with CLA-affiliated offices, providing valuable hands-on experience in professional services. Additionally, the recognition associated with the scholarship can strengthen future applications for jobs, graduate programs, and other competitive opportunities. Who Can Apply The scholarship is open to students in the United States who meet the following criteria: Applicants must be high school seniors, graduates, or current undergraduate or graduate students planning to enroll full-time in an accredited institution for the upcoming academic year. Eligible fields of study include accounting, business, finance, and a wide range of emerging disciplines such as computer science, data analytics, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and related areas. Applicants must have a minimum GPA of 2.5 on a 4.0 scale or equivalent. Candidates should also demonstrate leadership, participation in school or community activities, work experience, and clear educational and career goals. Financial need is considered as part of the selection process. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must complete the online application through the official Scholarship America platform. The application requires submission of a complete academic transcript, a personal statement outlining educational and career goals, and one recommendation form. Applicants must ensure all materials are submitted electronically before the deadline. Incomplete applications will not be considered. The deadline to apply is May 19, 2026, at 3:00 PM CT . Applicants will be notified of the results in July, and scholarship funds will be distributed in early August. This scholarship provides a valuable opportunity for students to invest in their education, build their professional future, and contribute to the evolving world of professional services. To learn mire and apply, visit the Scholarship America website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the CLA Foundation Scholarship 2026 and Receive $15,000 for Your Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply to Join the AWARD Reviewer Pool and Shape Africa’s Future Leaders in Agrifood Systems
summary: The AWARD Reviewer Pool offers experienced professionals the opportunity to contribute to the selection of Africa’s next generation of leaders in agrifood systems while gaining insight into emerging innovations in climate resilience and gender equality. Applications are open until 30 April 2026 for experts interested in supporting high-impact fellowship programs across the continent. African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) is inviting qualified professionals to join its reviewer pool for flagship fellowship initiatives. This opportunity is designed for individuals with strong expertise in agriculture, policy, climate change, and leadership development who are committed to advancing inclusive and sustainable development across Africa. About the Program The AWARD Reviewer Pool plays a critical role in maintaining the quality and integrity of fellowship selection processes. Reviewers are responsible for evaluating applications submitted by candidates across AWARD’s programs, which aim to build leadership capacity and strengthen agrifood systems throughout Africa. Rather than being a full-time role, this opportunity is a flexible, short-term engagement that allows professionals to contribute alongside their existing responsibilities. Selected reviewers will assess a small number of applications using structured evaluation criteria and provide objective, constructive feedback to support fair decision-making. The reviewer role also includes participation in virtual briefing sessions and adherence to clear guidelines on confidentiality, ethics, and conflict of interest. Through this process, AWARD ensures that its fellowship cohorts are composed of high-potential individuals capable of driving meaningful change. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa faces complex challenges related to climate change, food security, and sustainable agriculture. Addressing these challenges requires strong leadership, innovation, and inclusive approaches that prioritize gender equality and community resilience. By joining the AWARD Reviewer Pool, professionals play a direct role in identifying and supporting individuals who are working to transform agrifood systems across the continent. This contribution helps ensure that fellowship programs select candidates with the potential to drive impactful solutions in research, policy, and practice. Additionally, the reviewer pool strengthens the credibility and transparency of selection processes. By involving diverse experts with regional and thematic knowledge, AWARD enhances the quality of its programs and promotes equity in access to opportunities. What Participants Will Gain Serving as a reviewer offers both professional and intellectual benefits. Participants gain exposure to innovative ideas, research proposals, and emerging leaders working in agrifood systems and climate adaptation. Reviewers also deepen their analytical and evaluation skills by engaging with structured assessment frameworks and providing detailed feedback. This experience can strengthen their expertise in selection processes, grant evaluation, and program design. In addition, reviewers become part of a broader community of professionals committed to advancing sustainable development in Africa. This engagement can lead to new collaborations, knowledge exchange, and long-term involvement in AWARD’s initiatives. A modest honorarium is provided in recognition of the time and expertise contributed by reviewers. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to professionals with strong experience in areas such as agricultural research, agrifood systems, climate change, policy, gender, and leadership development. Applicants should hold a Master’s degree, PhD, or equivalent experience, along with demonstrated expertise in research, academia, policy, or program leadership. Strong analytical and critical thinking skills are essential, as well as the ability to provide objective and high-quality assessments. Experience reviewing proposals, fellowships, or grants is considered an advantage, as is proficiency in English and/or French. Application Process and Deadline Interested candidates must submit their application through the official Reviewer Expression of Interest form available online. Applications submitted through other means will not be considered. The deadline to apply is 30 April 2026 . Selected reviewers will be contacted with further details regarding assignments, timelines, and onboarding processes. This is a valuable opportunity for experienced professionals to contribute their expertise to high-impact programs while supporting the development of future leaders working to address some of Africa’s most pressing challenges. To learn more and apply, visit the official African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Join DC Action’s Board of Directors: Lead Change for Youth in Washington, DC
summary: The DC Action Young Adult Board Member opportunity offers young leaders aged 18–30 the chance to gain governance experience while shaping policies that impact children and youth across Washington, DC. This unique leadership role is designed for individuals who are passionate about advocacy, equity, and community impact, and who want to contribute their voice to decision-making processes that influence real change. DC Action is currently seeking applications from motivated young adults ready to step into leadership roles and support its mission of ensuring that all children and youth in the District of Columbia grow up safe, resilient, and empowered. About the Program DC Action is a nonprofit organization dedicated to improving outcomes for children and youth through research, advocacy, and collaboration. Formed through the merger of DC Action for Children and the DC Alliance of Youth Advocates, the organization works to unify efforts across sectors and ensure a more coordinated approach to supporting young people. As a Young Adult Board Member, selected candidates will serve on the organization’s Board of Directors and contribute to governance, oversight, and strategic decision-making. Board members play an important role in guiding the organization’s direction, ensuring accountability, and strengthening its advocacy efforts. This position is designed not only to support the organization but also to provide young leaders with hands-on experience in nonprofit governance and policy engagement. Why This Opportunity Matters Young people are often the most affected by policies related to education, housing, health, and economic opportunity, yet they are frequently underrepresented in decision-making spaces. This opportunity addresses that gap by placing young leaders directly at the table where key decisions are made. By joining the DC Action Board, young adults can bring their lived experiences and perspectives into discussions that shape programs and policies. This contributes to more inclusive, equitable, and effective solutions for communities. In addition, the organization’s commitment to racial equity and its focus on addressing systemic barriers ensures that board members are engaging in work that has long-term impact on social justice and community wellbeing. What Participants Will Gain Serving as a Young Adult Board Member offers both personal and professional development opportunities. Participants will gain valuable leadership experience by contributing to governance and strategic planning processes. They will also build skills in advocacy, event planning, and fundraising—key competencies for careers in nonprofit leadership, public policy, and community development. Board members will have the opportunity to expand their professional networks, collaborate with experienced leaders, and gain insight into how organizations operate at a strategic level. In addition, selected participants will receive a modest quarterly stipend, providing financial support while they contribute their time and expertise. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to young adults aged 18 to 30 who are passionate about improving outcomes for children and youth in Washington, DC. Applicants should demonstrate a strong interest in advocacy, leadership, and community engagement. While prior board experience is not required, candidates should be committed to contributing actively to the organization’s mission and participating in governance activities. DC Action encourages applications from individuals with diverse backgrounds and perspectives, particularly those from communities that are directly impacted by systemic inequities. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must submit a resume and a cover letter outlining their interest, experience, and motivation for joining the board. Review the official requirements and responsibilities on the DC Action website . The deadline to apply is 15 May 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to submit thoughtful and well-prepared applications that clearly demonstrate their commitment to youth advocacy and leadership. This role provides a meaningful pathway for young leaders to influence policy, build skills, and contribute to a stronger future for children and youth in Washington, DC. title: Fully Funded Green RISE Africa Fellowship 2026: Empowering Climate Entrepreneurs Across Africa
summary: Looking to take your climate-focused enterprise to the next level? The Acumen Academy is now accepting applications for the Green RISE Africa Fellowship 2026. This fully funded leadership program supports entrepreneurs across East and West Africa who are building solutions at the intersection of climate action and poverty reduction. Designed for emerging leaders driving inclusive green growth, the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to strengthen your leadership, scale your enterprise, and join a global community of changemakers. Applications open on 11 May 2026 , with the program starting in July 2026. About the Opportunity The Green RISE Africa Fellowship (Resilient Impact through Sustainable Entrepreneurship) is a six-month hybrid leadership program that equips entrepreneurs with the tools, frameworks, and networks needed to grow sustainable, climate-aligned businesses. Unlike traditional business programs, this fellowship focuses on moral leadership —helping participants develop the values, mindset, and decision-making skills required to create long-term, inclusive impact. Fellows remain in their current roles while engaging in virtual learning, in-person immersives, and peer collaboration. The program is designed to support entrepreneurs who are not only building businesses, but also creating dignified work opportunities , particularly for women and youth in underserved communities. East vs West Africa Fellowship The fellowship is delivered through two regional tracks, each tailored to specific contexts while maintaining a shared mission of building an inclusive green economy. West Africa Fellowship This track focuses on entrepreneurs working to expand green job opportunities for young rural women and youth across West Africa. It emphasizes building enterprises rooted in dignity, equity, and sustainable growth. Participants engage in immersive learning experiences, including in-person sessions in Nigeria , while connecting with a diverse network of peers tackling similar challenges across the region. East Africa Fellowship The East Africa track is open to entrepreneurs in countries such as Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, Ethiopia, Burundi, South Sudan, and Somalia. It focuses on strengthening climate-aligned business models , improving resilience, and scaling impact across communities. Fellows participate in in-person immersives in Kenya and benefit from access to regional and global expertise. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa is at the forefront of both climate vulnerability and climate innovation. Entrepreneurs across the continent are developing solutions in renewable energy, agriculture, waste management, and more—but often face barriers in scaling their impact. The Green RISE Africa Fellowship addresses this gap by combining leadership development with practical enterprise support . It helps participants navigate complex challenges, engage stakeholders effectively, and build systems that prioritize both environmental sustainability and social inclusion. By fostering a strong community of leaders, the program also creates opportunities for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and long-term partnerships that extend far beyond the fellowship itself. What You’ll Gain Participants benefit from a comprehensive and fully funded experience, including: A structured 6-month leadership development program Access to Acumen’s global alumni network, The Foundry (2,000+ members) Training in key areas such as adaptive leadership, storytelling, and inclusive business strategies Participation in virtual sessions and in-person immersives Personalized mentorship and peer learning opportunities Continued engagement in a climate-focused community of practice after the program In addition, fellows may gain access to future funding and growth opportunities to further scale their enterprises. Program Structure The fellowship is designed to be flexible, requiring approximately 10 hours per month . It includes: A 3-day virtual orientation A 2-day virtual workshop Two 5-day in-person immersives Monthly cohort sessions and learning labs Self-paced assignments and one-on-one support This structure allows participants to immediately apply what they learn to their own enterprises. Eligibility This opportunity is ideal for individuals who: Are building or leading a green enterprise Focus on creating inclusive economic opportunities Are committed to addressing climate change and poverty Demonstrate leadership potential and a strong vision for impact Are based in eligible East or West African countries Application Process and Timeline To apply, candidates must: Review the Applicant Toolkit Submit an online application (opens 11 May 2026) Participate in a Selection Conference (4–6 June 2026) if shortlisted Begin the fellowship in July 2026 If you are ready to grow your enterprise and contribute to Africa’s green future, visit the official website to apply: See the West Africa Fellowship See the East Africa Fellowship Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: UN Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Programme Management 2026 – Fully Funded International Career Opportunity in New York
summary: If you are a young professional seeking a high-impact global career with the United Nations, the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Programme Management offers one of the most prestigious entry points into international development. Sponsored by the Government of the Netherlands and coordinated through the Nedworc Foundation , this opportunity places talented individuals at the heart of global policy and development coordination. What is the JPO Programme and Why It Matters The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is a globally recognized career pathway into the United Nations system. It is specifically designed to recruit and train young professionals who demonstrate strong academic backgrounds and a passion for global development. Participants gain: Hands-on experience within UN agencies Exposure to high-level policy processes Access to global professional networks Structured training and career development opportunities The programme plays a critical role in supporting the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, enabling professionals to contribute directly to global change. Detailed Job Description and Responsibilities As a JPO in Programme Management, you will work within the Office of the Assistant Secretary-General for Development Coordination. This role is deeply embedded in the strategic operations of the United Nations. Core Responsibilities Include: Coordinating contributions to high-level UN leadership briefings Drafting reports, background documents, and policy papers Supporting programme development, implementation, and evaluation Conducting research and analysis on global development issues Assisting in policy development and strategic planning Organizing meetings, conferences, and consultations Monitoring programme progress and recommending improvements You will also collaborate with multiple UN entities, including regional teams and partner organizations, ensuring integrated development coordination across the system. Eligibility Criteria – Who Can Apply This opportunity is highly competitive and has strict eligibility requirements. Age Requirement Must be 32 years or younger by 31 December of the application year Eligible Countries Applicants must be citizens of one of the following countries: Africa: Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Ethiopia, Uganda, Senegal, Sudan, South Sudan, Somalia, Mozambique, Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Chad, Benin, Ivory Coast Asia Middle East: India, Indonesia, Iraq, Jordan, Lebanon, Yemen, Bangladesh, Vietnam Others: Colombia, Ukraine, Palestinian Territory This means candidates from Africa and other developing regions are strongly encouraged to apply, making it a highly inclusive opportunity. Educational Qualifications To qualify, candidates must meet the following academic requirements: A Master’s degree in: Public Administration International Relations Business Management Or a closely related field Alternative Pathway: A Bachelor’s degree + minimum 4 years of relevant experience may be accepted Additionally, part of your academic studies must have taken place in an eligible country. Professional Experience Requirements While this is an entry-level professional role, candidates are expected to demonstrate: Strong analytical and research skills Experience in programme/project management Ability to draft reports and policy documents Knowledge of international development frameworks Relevant work or internship experience in international organizations, NGOs, or government institutions is highly advantageous. Training, Benefits, and Career Growth The JPO Programme is not just a job—it is a structured career development platform. Key Benefits Include: Participation in the UN JPO Orientation Programme in Turin (Italy) Annual $4,000 training and travel allowance Access to UN internal learning and development programmes Direct mentorship and supervision by senior UN officials Opportunity to transition into long-term UN careers By the end of the programme, participants gain: Advanced knowledge of UN systems and operations Experience in global policy coordination Strong professional networks within the UN system Who Should Apply (Target Audience) This opportunity is ideal for: Young professionals passionate about global development Graduates in international relations, public policy, or related fields Individuals seeking careers in the United Nations or multilateral organizations Candidates from developing countries looking for global exposure Why This Opportunity Stands Out Unlike many international roles, this JPO position: Is fully integrated into the UN system Offers direct exposure to senior leadership processes Provides structured training and mentorship Targets talent from developing countries, including Africa It is one of the few global career opportunities that combines policy influence, international exposure, and long-term career prospects. How to Apply Interested applicants must submit their applications through the official UN application portal. Before applying: Carefully review the eligibility criteria Prepare academic and professional documents Read the full job description and FAQ on the Nedworc Foundation website Important: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted, and late applications will not be considered. Apply here Deadline 26th April 2026 The UN JPO in Programme Management 2026 is a transformative opportunity for young professionals aiming to build a meaningful international career. With its strong focus on leadership development, policy engagement, and global impact, this programme serves as a gateway into the United Nations system. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post UN Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Programme Management 2026 – Fully Funded International Career Opportunity in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026: Guaranteed Internship, Global Career Path, and Hands-On Training in Europe
summary: The International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026, organized by Pixel in Florence, Italy, is a career-focused postgraduate programme designed to equip graduates and young professionals with the practical skills, global exposure, and professional experience needed to succeed in international project management roles. This unique master’s programme stands out for its guaranteed internship component, strong employment outcomes, and hands-on approach to learning. With over 250 alumni from more than 40 countries and an 85% employment rate in international project roles, the programme has established itself as a leading pathway into careers in European and global cooperation projects. About the Master Programme The Master in European Project Planning and Management is a specialized training programme aimed at preparing participants for careers in international development, European Union-funded projects, and global cooperation initiatives. The programme integrates: Academic learning Practical project simulations Real-world internship experience It is designed to bridge the gap between academic education and professional employment by focusing on applied skills and direct industry exposure. Why Choose This Master Programme The programme offers a number of distinctive advantages that make it highly competitive and career-oriented. Key Reasons to Apply Guaranteed International Internship All participants receive a 3-month internship placement Internships available in over 60 organizations across 20+ European countries Roles typically include Assistant European Project Manager Strong Career Outcomes 85% of alumni are employed in international project roles Graduates work across Europe and globally Hands-On Learning Approach Real project simulations Case studies and applied learning Practical exposure to EU funding systems International Learning Environment Participants from over 40 countries Global network of students and professionals Expert Faculty Trainers with 25–30 years of experience Professionals in project planning, evaluation, and auditing Institutional Expertise Organized by Pixel, with over 27 years of experience in European projects and international training Programme Structure The Master programme is divided into two main modules, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of both project planning and management. Module 1: European Project Planning This module focuses on developing strong project design and proposal skills. Topics Covered European policies and funding opportunities Identifying and matching ideas with funding programmes Understanding calls for proposals Project idea development Transnational partnership creation Budget planning and financial development Application form preparation Project quality and evaluation criteria Module 2: European Project Management This module focuses on implementing and managing projects effectively. Topics Covered Project management cycle Contractual procedures and amendments Time management and planning tools Logical Framework Approach Risk management and quality assurance Leadership and communication Monitoring and evaluation Financial and administrative management Reporting and documentation Internship Programme A key feature of the Master is its guaranteed internship component. Internship Details Duration: 3 months Placement: 60+ partner organizations Countries: 20+ European countries Roles: Assistant European Project Manager Internship Formats Onsite internships in Europe Online internships for flexible participation Internships are tailored to each participant’s profile, ensuring alignment with career goals and interests. Skills Acquired By the end of the programme, participants will gain a wide range of professional competencies. Core Skills Project planning and proposal development Budget preparation and financial management Partnership coordination Project implementation and monitoring Reporting and evaluation Communication and leadership Career Opportunities Graduates of the programme go on to work in a variety of roles and sectors. Career Roles European Project Manager Project Officer International Cooperation Officer Research Project Manager Employment Sectors Universities and research centres NGOs and international organizations Public institutions and EU bodies Private companies and consultancies Programme Format and Duration Key Details Duration: 5 months (800 hours) Training Phase: 2 months Internship Phase: 3 months Language: English Study Options Participants can choose between: Onsite Programme Location: Florence, Italy Immersive international experience Online Programme Flexible learning format Access to recordings and tutor support Start Dates Synchronous (Onsite/Online): Mid-October Asynchronous Online: Available anytime Eligibility Criteria The programme is open to: Recent graduates Young professionals International applicants Requirements University degree or diploma Good command of English Tuition Fees Online Programme: €2,900 Onsite Programme: €3,800 About Pixel Pixel is an international education and training institution based in Florence, Italy, with extensive experience in: European project planning International cooperation Higher education training Participants benefit from Pixel’s strong network and expertise in EU-funded projects. How to Apply Applicants can: Book a consultation call Apply through the Official Website Application Link Apply for the Master Programme Why You Should Apply This programme is ideal for individuals seeking to: Build a career in international project management Gain hands-on experience in European projects Access global job opportunities Develop practical and transferable skills Join a global professional network Click HERE to read more about the International Master in European Project Planning and Management 2026. Visit OFY for more other global Internship and Scholarship opportunities. title: Catalyst Academy 2026: Youth Social Innovation Program in Africa (Apply Now)
summary: The Catalyst Academy is a regional youth social innovation learning program implemented across Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Cameroon, and South Africa. Led by entrepreneurs and academics from Catalyst Now , the program is designed to equip young people with the skills, networks, and opportunities needed to thrive as social innovators. Through peer learning circles, technology-enabled platforms, and cross-sector collaboration, the program engages youth participants in co-creating solutions to pressing community challenges. Participants gain access to mentorship from experienced social innovators across Africa and beyond, alongside a structured learning journey that includes facilitated sessions, mentorship, and continuous peer exchange. Who Can Apply The program is open to: Young aspiring social entrepreneurs aged 18–35 years Individuals interested in social innovation Young women facing barriers to entrepreneurship Youth, especially persons living with disabilities or refugees/displaced persons seeking dignified employment opportunities Community leaders aiming to scale their social impact Applicants must be nationals or residents of one of the following countries: Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Cameroon, or South Africa. Program Structure and Support The Catalyst Academy is designed to provide both direct support to participants and wider community impact through knowledge sharing and innovation. Direct Participant Support Includes: Weekly peer learning sessions to build knowledge, skills, and confidence Connection to experienced mentors in relevant fields Full access to a digital learning platform and curated resources Opportunities to participate in cross-border online learning exchanges Entry into the Catalyst Now network and alumni community What Participants Will Gain Participants in the program will: Build a real-world project addressing a community challenge Collaborate in small teams with other young changemakers Learn through hands-on, experiential learning rather than theory alone Develop and test a pilot solution rather than only conceptual ideas Application Note Applicants are encouraged to carefully complete all sections of the application, as the form includes multiple detailed questions. Candidates should provide authentic and personal responses. The application is an opportunity for selection teams to understand each applicant’s motivations, experiences, and ideas, so generic or AI-generated responses should be avoided. CLICK HERE TO APPLY Deadline The deadline to submit applications is 20 April 2026 . Discover similar opportunities Here. title: Fully Funded EU Traineeship for Young Graduates in Nigeria 2026
summary: If you are a young graduate looking to build a career in international relations, diplomacy, public policy, or global affairs, the Funded Traineeship for Young Graduates at the EU Delegation to the Federal Republic of Nigeria and ECOWAS 2026 is one of the most prestigious opportunities currently open. This comprehensive guide provides everything you need to know—from eligibility and benefits to application steps and deadlines—structured to help you successfully apply and stand out. About the EU Traineeship Program The traineeship is hosted by the EU Delegation in Nigeria, which represents the European Union’s interests and values in the country and within the regional bloc ECOWAS. The program is specifically designed for young graduates interested in international relations and diplomacy. Participants will be placed in the Political, Press, and Information (PPI) Section, where they will gain hands-on experience in diplomatic work, policy analysis, and international cooperation. This traineeship provides a rare opportunity to work within a global diplomatic mission and understand how international relations operate in real-world settings. Detailed Description of the Role The traineeship focuses on supporting the Political, Press, and Information Section of the EU Delegation. This department plays a critical role in: Managing political relations between the EU and Nigeria Monitoring regional developments in West Africa and ECOWAS Engaging with governments, civil society, and media Supporting diplomatic coordination among EU Member States Key Responsibilities Selected trainees will: Draft analytical reports on political, economic, and social developments Monitor human rights and regional policy issues Assist in organizing meetings and preparing minutes Support communication and public diplomacy activities Help coordinate official visits from EU institutions Contribute to policy discussions and research initiatives This broad exposure ensures participants gain both analytical and practical diplomatic experience. Benefits of the EU Funded Traineeship Although specific financial details may vary, EU traineeships typically offer: A monthly stipend (approximately €700) to support living expenses Hands-on experience in a high-level diplomatic environment Exposure to international policymaking and governance Networking opportunities with global professionals A strong career boost for roles in international organizations, NGOs, or government Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) This opportunity is highly specific in its eligibility criteria, meaning only certain applicants qualify. Eligible Category of People Young graduates interested in international relations Individuals seeking early-career experience in diplomacy Nationality Requirement Must be a citizen of Nigeria Residency Requirement Applicants must currently reside in Abuja, Nigeria due to local conditions Educational and Professional Qualifications To be eligible, applicants must meet the following requirements: Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) Have less than one year of professional experience after graduation Have no prior experience exceeding six weeks in EU institutions Important note: Internships, volunteering, or student jobs do not count as professional experience. Countries and Regions Eligible Eligible Country: Nigeria only Eligible Continent: Africa (restricted to Nigerian citizens) This is not a global program—it is specifically targeted at local Nigerian graduates residing in Abuja. Required Documents for Application Applicants must prepare and submit the following: A Europass CV (mandatory format) A cover letter explaining motivation for applying A completed application form Applications submitted in non-Europass CV formats will not be considered. How to Apply (Step-by-Step Guide) Prepare your Europass CV using the official EU template Write a strong, tailored cover letter highlighting your interest in diplomacy Complete the official traineeship application form Send all documents via email to: Delegation-Nigeria-Recruitment@eeas.europa.eu Use a clear subject line indicating the traineeship Apply here Why This Opportunity Matters This EU traineeship stands out because: It provides direct exposure to international diplomacy It allows you to work with one of the most influential global institutions It builds practical policy analysis and communication skills It significantly enhances your career prospects in global organizations For many participants, this experience becomes a stepping stone into careers with the European Union, United Nations, or other international bodies. Deadline 19th April 2026 The EU Delegation Traineeship in Nigeria 2026 is a highly competitive and career-defining opportunity for young Nigerian graduates. With its strong focus on diplomacy, international relations, and policy engagement, it offers unparalleled exposure to the inner workings of global governance. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded EU Traineeship for Young Graduates in Nigeria 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Now Open: CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Leaders in Urban Planning & Sustainability
summary: If you’re passionate about shaping stronger communities while building a meaningful career in public service, the CivicSpark Fellowship 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to do both. This award-winning program connects emerging leaders with local governments and community organizations to tackle pressing community planning challenges. Fellow Applications Deadline: May 22 at 5 PM PT What is CivicSpark? CivicSpark is an evidence-based fellowship program designed to strengthen local workforce capacity while advancing critical community planning priorities. Through hands-on service, fellows gain real-world experience and contribute to impactful projects that improve local communities. About the Fellowship Each year, CivicSpark recruits over 100 emerging leaders eager to develop professional skills and make a difference. During the 11-month service term , fellows: Conduct research and data analysis Support planning and policy development Implement community-based projects Collaborate with local governments and organizations Fellows work on initiatives such as: Waste management systems Urban and regional planning Sustainability and environmental programs Community engagement strategies Why Join CivicSpark? For Fellows Gain hands-on experience in public sector work Build a strong professional network Develop leadership and project management skills Make a measurable impact in local communities For Host Organizations Receive dedicated support on community planning projects Increase the likelihood of achieving project goals Build internal capacity and workforce development Organizations that partner with CivicSpark are 5 to 15 times more likely to achieve their project goals compared to those without a fellow. CivicSpark Impact at a Glance 12 Years of building local workforce capacity 1.25 million hours of service delivered 1,700 community events hosted 110,000+ community members engaged 300 local public agencies supported 1,000 emerging leaders trained 73% of alumni continue working in the public sector 95%+ of projects meet or exceed expectations Active in California, Colorado, and Washington , with expansion opportunities Awards Recognition CivicSpark’s impact has been widely recognized, including: 2024 Segal AmeriCorps Alums Segal Leadership Award 2020 Governor’s Office of Business Economic Development – ZEVe Award 2018 Governor’s Volunteering Service Award (Agency of the Year) 2017 Service Year Impact Award (Mid-Size Program) How to Apply Become a Fellow If you re ready to launch your career while making a difference, apply to become a CivicSpark Fellow before May 22 at 5 PM PT . Click Here , on the application proces For more details about this Fellowship, click Here Host a Fellow Organizations seeking support for community planning projects can apply to host a fellow by May 14 . Whether you re an aspiring civic leader or a public agency looking to amplify your impact, CivicSpark offers a proven pathway to success. By bridging talent with community needs, the program continues to transform both careers and communities. Apply now and be part of a movement driving real change. title: ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece: Leadership Programme on Geopolitics, Diplomacy, and Global Cooperation for Emerging Leaders
summary: The 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026, titled “Leadership Beyond Boundaries: Empowering the Next Generation in an Age of Geopolitical Flux,” is a prestigious international training programme designed for emerging leaders, policymakers, and professionals seeking to deepen their understanding of global geopolitics and leadership in a rapidly changing world. Organized by the Hellenic Foundation for European and Foreign Policy (ELIAMEP) in collaboration with the Institute of Global Affairs at The American College of Greece (ACG), the Summer Academy will take place from July 13 to July 17, 2026, in Ancient Olympia, Greece—one of the world’s most symbolic locations for peace, cooperation, and global dialogue. This immersive programme brings together participants from across the Mediterranean, Europe, and beyond to engage in high-level discussions, interactive workshops, and practical training sessions on pressing global and regional challenges. About the ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy The ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy is a cross-border initiative that has grown into a highly respected platform for leadership development and policy dialogue. Over the years, it has successfully convened graduate students, young professionals, and rising leaders to examine critical issues shaping international relations and regional stability. Participants benefit from: Direct engagement with leading academics and policymakers Exposure to real-world geopolitical challenges Opportunities to build international networks Development of leadership and negotiation skills Programme Overview Key Details Programme Title: Leadership Beyond Boundaries: Empowering the Next Generation in an Age of Geopolitical Flux Dates: July 13 – July 17, 2026 Location: International Olympic Academy, Ancient Olympia, Greece Language of Instruction: English Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 The programme is delivered fully in person and requires full participation throughout its duration. Programme Structure and Learning Experience The Summer Academy offers an immersive and interactive learning environment that combines theory with practice. Learning Components Participants will engage in: Expert-led lectures Policy discussions and debates Case studies and scenario analysis Interactive workshops Networking sessions Core Training Areas The programme emphasizes leadership development through: Conflict management Negotiation techniques Strategic foresight International cooperation These components are designed to equip participants with practical tools for navigating complex global environments. Key Themes of the Programme The Summer Academy addresses critical geopolitical and economic issues affecting the Mediterranean region and the broader global system. 1. Global Geopolitical and Geoeconomic Shifts Participants will explore: Changing global power dynamics Economic competition and alliances Impacts on regional and global stability Emerging opportunities and risks 2. Role of Global and Regional Powers This theme examines how major actors influence the Mediterranean region, including: European Union United States Russia China Middle East and Gulf countries Participants will analyze how these actors shape cooperation, competition, and governance. 3. Europe’s Strategic Interests Key focus areas include: Security and defense Energy and sustainability Migration and mobility Trade and economic resilience Participants will gain insight into EU policies and strategic frameworks. 4. Conflict Management and Policy Bridges This theme focuses on: Understanding conflict drivers Diplomatic solutions and negotiation strategies Inclusive governance models Multilateral cooperation Interactive Workshops Participants will develop essential professional skills through specialized workshops, including: Strategic Foresight Skills Advanced Negotiation Techniques Public Speaking and Media Training These workshops are designed to enhance leadership capacity and communication effectiveness. Target Participants The programme is designed for individuals with a strong interest in international affairs and leadership. Eligible Profiles Include Junior political and policy practitioners Think tank researchers and analysts Civil society representatives Diplomats Journalists Postgraduate students Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Hold at least a postgraduate or Master’s degree in a relevant field Be under 35 years of age Demonstrate leadership potential Have proficiency in English Be available for the full duration of the programme Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents in English: Statement of Purpose (maximum 200 words) Updated CV Proof of English proficiency The statement of purpose should clearly demonstrate how the programme aligns with the applicant’s professional goals. Click HERE to submit your application. Participation Fee and What It Covers Programme Fee €450 Included in the Fee Accommodation (full-board) Local transportation Not Included International travel costs For additional inquiries, please contact: SummerAcademy@eliamep.gr Venue and Learning Environment The programme will take place at the International Olympic Academy in Ancient Olympia, Greece—a historic and symbolic location associated with peace, unity, and global cooperation. This setting provides a unique and inspiring environment for learning, dialogue, and collaboration. Why You Should Apply The ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy offers a valuable opportunity to: Gain deep insights into global geopolitics Develop leadership and negotiation skills Learn from leading experts and practitioners Build an international professional network Enhance career prospects in policy, diplomacy, and international relations Click HERE to read more about the 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026. Discover more other global Conference opportunities on OFY HERE.
